title: Fix Admin Panel link not appearing in Navbar

requirements.text: In frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, fix the `useIsCallerAdmin` query so it: (1) is enabled only when the user is authenticated (identity is available), (2) uses the authenticated actor (not anonymous) to call the backend `isCallerAdmin()` function, (3) includes the identity/principal as a query key dependency so it re-fetches automatically when the user logs in or out, and (4) has a short staleTime (e.g. 0 or 5000ms) so it re-fetches on mount after login.

quotes: admin panel not show in navbar, please solve this issue

acceptanceCriteria: useIsCallerAdmin calls isCallerAdmin() on the authenticated backend actor
The query key includes the user's principal or identity so it invalidates on login/logout
The query is disabled when no identity is present (unauthenticated)
After admin logs in, the query re-fetches and returns true without requiring a page reload

requirements.text: In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, fix the Admin Panel link visibility: (1) call `useIsCallerAdmin` at the top of the component, (2) after login completes, ensure the query result is awaited before deciding whether to show the link, (3) conditionally render the 'Admin Panel' link (navigating to /admin) in both the desktop nav and mobile hamburger menu only when `isLoggedIn === true` AND `isCallerAdmin === true`, (4) do not render the link during the loading state of the admin check to avoid flicker.

quotes: admin panel not show in navbar, please solve this issue

acceptanceCriteria: Admin Panel link is visible in the desktop navbar when the admin user is logged in
Admin Panel link is visible in the mobile hamburger menu when the admin user is logged in
Admin Panel link is NOT visible for non-admin authenticated users
Admin Panel link is NOT visible for unauthenticated users
Link does not flicker or briefly appear for non-admins during the loading state

requirements.text: In frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, verify that when the user logs in via Internet Identity, the authenticated actor is created and cached correctly with the new identity, and that React Query is notified to invalidate or refetch queries that depend on the authenticated actor (including `isCallerAdmin`). If query invalidation is not triggered after login, add an explicit `queryClient.invalidateQueries` call targeting the admin check query key upon identity change.

quotes: admin panel not show in navbar, please solve this issue

acceptanceCriteria: After login, the authenticated actor is immediately available for backend calls
The isCallerAdmin query is invalidated/refetched after a new identity is set
The Admin Panel link appears in the navbar within one render cycle after the admin logs in

constraints: Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Do not change the backend Motoko logic or data models
Do not alter timer, navigation, or test submission logic in Test.tsx
Preserve all existing tab functionality in Admin.tsx
nonGoals: Adding new navbar links beyond Home, Dashboard, and Admin Panel
Changing the Admin Panel page content or tabs
Any changes to question, test, or result data models
